title: OOF Discrete Stint Hazard Priors
summary: Pit windows are naturally discrete hazards: given the stint survived to tyre age t, what is the chance of stopping on the next lap?
rationale: Pit windows are naturally discrete hazards: given the stint survived to tyre age t, what is the chance of stopping on the next lap? Discrete-time hazard literature and AWS’s Pit Window framing support replacing hand-set compound life maps with learned survival/hazard priors.
implementation_hint: On each training fold only, estimate hazard and survival tables for stint end by TyreLife conditioned on Compound, wet/dry, Stint, and coarse race-length or progress bins. Add features such as hazard_now, survival_3laps, and excess_hazard_vs_global, with smoothing toward broader parent groups when cells are sparse.
expected_effect: Should outperform fixed tyre-life heuristics by learning real compound-specific and regime-specific stop cliffs.
risk: Sparse cells can overfit, especially if you condition too hard on track-year; tables must be computed out-of-fold by held-out races.
